behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily in backoff math/state progression within one retry policy object. REAL-INFRA: none required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not hide the core defect because delay computation itself is the behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff policy function/object with consecutive failure counts (e.g., 1..N), asserting delays follow exponential growth and clamp at maxDelay; include boundary at first clamp and post-clamp attempts.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is at a module boundary (HTTP response classification + retry scheduler/backoff). Unit tests of classifier alone can miss wiring/field-mapping defects. REAL-INFRA: real network not required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware with a fake transport returning concrete rate-limit responses (e.g., 429 plus headers/body variants). Assert retrier classifies as quota/rate-limit and schedules the expected delay source (header-driven or policy fallback).

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when retry orchestrator, timer abstraction, and request executor interact (resend same request, preserve result path). Unit-testing one function is insufficient. REAL-INFRA: can be done with fake clock/transport.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process client with fake clock and scripted transport: first call returns rate-limited response, second returns success. Advance clock by required delay, assert transport was invoked twice with equivalent request payload, and `call()` resolves to second response data without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error guarantee is at the public `call()` boundary after retry exhaustion, requiring retry loop + error mapping to work together. Unit-level error-class test can miss integration path returning generic errors. REAL-INFRA: none required.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low `maxRetries`, fake transport always returns rate-limit. Execute `call()` and assert rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` (instance/type guard), includes expected metadata (attempt count/quota context), and is not a generic Error from lower layers.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: indefinite-loop risk emerges from interaction of retry counter, quota tracker, and scheduler; not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network needed if clock and transport are controlled. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run client with fake clock and always-rate-limited transport; assert bounded number of attempts and terminal rejection after expected retry count/time budget. Add a hard test timeout/guard to detect non-termination and fail if attempt count exceeds expected cap.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: shared-quota correctness is a cross-caller state interaction bug (race/duplication) across modules; unit tests of quota bucket alone may miss client-level wiring and concurrency semantics. REAL-INFRA: in-process async concurrency is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/shared quota manager, issue concurrent `call()` invocations from multiple async tasks against a scripted transport and deterministic scheduler. Assert aggregate consumed tokens/slots reflect one shared bucket and per-caller outcomes match shared-limit contention rules.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering failures arise in queue + dispatcher + retry wake-up interaction, not isolated logic only. REAL-INFRA: no external system required; deterministic in-process scheduling can expose it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Queue multiple calls rapidly under forced wait conditions (quota exhausted then replenished/time advanced). With fake clock/transport, assert dispatch invocation order matches submission order (FIFO) across retries/wake-ups.
